title: TDFLite Policy Bundle
description: A sealed policy bundle for TDFLite. Contains attribute definitions, identity-to-attribute assignments, and (when sealed) encrypted KAS key material. Combined with an SSH key, one file replaces all config, provisioning scripts, and manual setup. See https://github.com/opentdf/tdflite for details.

version.description: Schema version number. Currently must be 1. Omitting defaults to 1.

namespace.description: Namespace for attribute fully-qualified names (FQNs). Attributes become: https://{namespace}/attr/{name}/value/{value}. Omitting defaults to "tdflite.local".

attributes.description: Policy attribute definitions. Each attribute has a name, an access rule type, and a list of allowed values. At least one attribute is required.

identities.description: Map of identity names to their attribute claims and optional credentials. Keys are usernames (e.g. "alice", "bob"). At least one identity is required.

options.description: Optional overrides for power users. Controls token TTL and default subject mapping actions.

sealed.description: Encrypted key material, present only after the bundle has been sealed with an SSH public key. Must be accompanied by a signature.

signature.description: Base64-encoded Ed25519 signature computed over all fields except the signature itself. Present only after sealing. Verified at boot to ensure the bundle has not been tampered with.

Attribute.description: A policy attribute definition specifying the attribute name, its access evaluation rule, and the set of allowed values.

name.description: Attribute identifier. Becomes part of the fully-qualified name: https://{namespace}/attr/{name}/value/{value}. Must contain only alphanumeric characters and underscores.

rule.description:
How the attribute's values are evaluated during access decisions.

- "hierarchy": Values are ordered by clearance level (first = highest). The user's level must be >= the data's level.
- "allOf": The user must possess ALL values tagged on the data.
- "anyOf": The user must possess AT LEAST ONE value tagged on the data.

values.description: Allowed values for this attribute. For hierarchy rules, order matters: the first element represents the highest clearance level. At least one value is required.

items.description: An individual attribute value. Must be a non-empty string.

Identity.description: Represents a user or service account identity. Contains optional credential overrides (admin, password, client_id, client_secret) and attribute claims as additional properties. Claim keys must match attribute names defined in the bundle's attributes array. Hierarchy attribute claims must be a single string value; allOf/anyOf attribute claims must be an array of strings.

admin.description: When true, grants the identity the platform admin role. Default: false.

password.description: Overrides the default password for this identity. Default: "{username}-secret".

client_id.description: Overrides the default OAuth2 client ID for this identity. Default: "{username}-client".

client_secret.description: Overrides the default OAuth2 client secret for this identity. Default: "{username}-secret".

additionalProperties.description: Attribute claim. The key must match an attribute name from the attributes array. The value type depends on the attribute's rule: a single string for hierarchy attributes, or an array of strings for allOf/anyOf attributes. All values must be members of the attribute's allowed values list.

oneOf.description: Scalar string claim value, used for hierarchy attributes.
Array of string claim values, used for allOf/anyOf attributes.

Options.description: Optional overrides for advanced configuration. All fields are optional.

token_ttl.description: Overrides the default IdP token time-to-live. Accepts Go duration syntax: a sequence of decimal numbers with unit suffixes (e.g. "10m" for 10 minutes, "1h" for 1 hour, "30s" for 30 seconds, "1h30m" for 1 hour 30 minutes).

default_actions.description: Overrides the actions applied to all auto-generated subject mappings. Default: ["read", "create"].

Sealed.description: Contains age-encrypted key material. Supports two modes: SSH key mode (recommended) where keys are encrypted to an SSH public key, and passphrase mode where keys are encrypted with a passphrase-derived key via scrypt. Present only in sealed bundles.

kas_keys.description: Age-encrypted RSA and EC private keys for the Key Access Server (KAS). Encrypted using either an SSH public key or a passphrase. Decrypted at boot.

idp_key.description: Age-encrypted private key used by the built-in IdP (idplite) to sign JWTs. Encrypted using the same method as kas_keys.

fingerprint.description: SSH public key fingerprint (e.g. "SHA256:xxxx") identifying which SSH key was used to encrypt the keys. Present only in SSH key mode. Empty or omitted in passphrase mode.

method.description: Sealing method. "ssh" (default) means keys are encrypted to an SSH public key. "passphrase" means keys are encrypted with a passphrase-derived key via scrypt. Omitted defaults to "ssh".

then.description: In passphrase mode, fingerprint is not required.

else.description: In SSH key mode, fingerprint is required to identify the unsealing key.
